title: Acts of Spatial Violation: The Politics of Space-Making inside the Palestinian Refugee Camp
creator: Samar Maqusi
subject: Refugee camps; Palestinian refugees; displacement; United Nations; spatial violation; violence; architectural scale; spatial interventions; installations
annotation: Dr. Samar Maqusi is a Research Associate at University College London’s Person-Environment-Activity Research Laboratory (PEARL). This piece looks into the politics of space-making inside Palestinian refugee camps.

title: The Great March of Return: An Organizer's Perspective
creator: Jehad Abusalim
subject: Great March of Return; Gaza Strip; Jerusalem; nonviolence; right of return; peaceful protest; disproportionate targeting
annotation: This essay explores the genesis of the Great March of Return in the context of a fragmented Palestinian body politic, blockade, and occupation, highlighting two major issues: the Palestinian refugee plight and the decade-long blockade on Gaza.

title: The Art of Resistance in the Palestinian Struggle Against Israel
creator: Eray ALIM
academic_discipline: Middle Eastern Studies; Palestine Studies; Art Criticism
subject: Street Art, Palestine, Israel, Resistance, Politics
annotation: This manuscript aims to assess Palestinian street art’s effectiveness as a resistance tool and political instrument in the struggle waged against Israel.

title: “Ramadan Does Not Come for Free”: Refusal as New and Ongoing in Palestine
creator: Rana Barakat
subject: refusal; Dignity and Hope Strike; Lifta Charitable Society; Nizar Bana; hip-hop; settler colonialism
annotation: Palestinian-American historian Rana Barakat writes about the 2021 uprisings as continuation (“ongoing”) of a politics of refusal that historically marked Palestinian resistance to settler colonialism and later the settler state. Drawing on her personal experience living in Ramallah during this period, she focuses on three cases from 2021: the Dignity and Hope Strike, the Lifta Charitable Society-Jerusalem’s action of collective return to Lifta, and Palestinian Authority security force’s murder of activist Nizar Bana. This short (4-page), accessible work of historical analysis requires some broader knowledge of Palestinian history for context.

title: Placemaking Interventions in Palestine as Demonstration Effects on the Ground
creator: Ahmad El-Atrash
subject: Palestine, placemaking, public space
annotation: Ahmad El-Atrash is a Palestinian spatial planner and urban development specialist. This paper explores how the urban development and rapid urbanization that the West Bank, including occupied East Jerusalem and Gaza Strip have recently encountered have adversely affected the quality and availability of open spaces inside the Palestinian urban and rural areas. Public spaces are fundamental in the lives of any community striving to achieve a sustainable and inclusive environment and improve the quality of life of its inhabitants.

title: The everyday art of resistance: Interpreting "resistancescapes" against urban violence in Palestine
creator: Dana Hasan, Sahera Bleibleh
academic_discipline: Geography; Urban Studies; History; Sociology; Political Science
subject: Everyday resistance; Graffiti; Palestine; Sumud; The Segregation Wall; Traumascape; Urban violence; Resistancescapes
annotation: This paper offers an in-depth reflection on the Palestinian graffiti on the Segregation Wall. Through ethnographic research, the study explores the role of Palestinian graffiti in transforming the Israeli-imposed traumascape into what we term a resistancescape. By provoking urban dialogues around trauma sites, Palestinian graffiti offers an example of creative everyday resistance, empowering Palestinians to claim their right to the city.

title: The Politics of Gaza Beach: At the Edge of the Two Intifadas
creator: Laura Junka
subject: First Intifada; Second Intifada; politics of representation; agency; subjectivity; Gaza beach;
annotation: In a comparative analysis of the images of the First (1987-1993) and Second Intifada (2000-2005), Laura Junka traces shifts in Palestinian politics of representation under occupation. title: Landscape as Resistance in the West Bank
creator: Hubert Murray
subject: Landscape; Resistance; Cultural Heritage; Environment
annotation: Hubert Murray, an American architect, explores  how under mounting political and ecological stress, environmentalists in the Palestinian village of Battir are working to protect a 4,000-year-old agricultural landscape.

title: Counter-Spaces as Resistance in Conflict Zones
creator: Nadera Shalhoub-Kevorkian
subject: Feminism; security; reasoning; house demolitions; gender violence; conflict zones; Palestinian women; Arab family; counter-spaces; family policy; social policy; war
annotation: Nadera Shalhoub-Kevorkian, a Palestinian scholar and citizen of Israel, brings the feminist lens into the analysis of Israeli policies in the Occupied Palestinian Territories. It examines the effect and legacy of policies inflicted by the doctrines of “no safe haven” and of “security reasoning” in relation to women, explores the way it shapes women's interactions with their family members, and discusses the ways in which continued and unpredictable political oppression creates an art of resistance with therapeutic qualities.

title: Public spaces in the occupied Palestinian territories
creator: Zahraa Zawawi, Eric Corijn, Bas Van Heur
subject: Public spaces; Power relations; Transformation; Production; Perception
annotation: Most research on the Palestinian-Israeli conflict has focused on macro and meso-levels of abstraction by exploring national territorial and urban scales. This article, however, takes a more micro-level approach by investigating one specific public space in detail. It analyses the transformation and use of Dawar, the main public space of the city of Nablus, during the First (1987–1993) and Second (2000–2005) Intifadas.

title: Rethinking Statehood in Palestine
creator: Leila Farsakh
academic_discipline: Palestine Studies; Political Science; History; Political Economy
subject: Resistance; History; Statehood; Indigeniety; Nationalism
annotation: The quest for an inclusive and independent state has been at the center of the Palestinian national struggle for a very long time. This book critically explores the meaning of Palestinian statehood and the challenges that face alternative models to it. Giving prominence to a young set of diverse Palestinian scholars, this groundbreaking book shows how notions of citizenship, sovereignty, and nationhood are being rethought within the broader context of decolonization. Bringing forth critical and multifaceted engagements with what modern Palestinian self-determination entails, Rethinking Statehood sets the terms of debate for the future of Palestine beyond partition.

title: Cultivating biodiverse futures at the (postcolonial) botanical garden
creator: Silvia Hassouna
journal_title: Transactions of the Institute of British Geographers
academic_discipline: Palestine Studies; Ecology; Environmental Science; Anthropology
subject: botanical garden; environmentalism; ethnography; futurity; Palestine; settler colonialism
annotation: This article examines ecological practices at the Palestine Museum of Natural History in Bethlehem, West Bank. Through an analysis of the museum's botanical gardens, the article explores what it calls ‘biodiverse futures’ as a spatio-temporal alternative to the ecological domination of settler colonialism in Israel/Palestine. While much scholarship has focused on the environmental imaginaries that have informed colonial conquest in Palestine, this paper draws attention to the ways in which these relationships extend into constructions of the future.
